ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic snack bar class; no specific KIND bar or dark chocolate nut bar entry exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKW covers chocolate-coated nut bars as the closest defensible category for a dark chocolate and nut snack bar product.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code exists for a commercial nut-and-chocolate snack bar. Omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

allergens: soy (dark chocolate may contain soy lecithin)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard KIND Dark Chocolate Nuts & Sea Salt Bar weighs approximately 40 g and is typically consumed as a single snack serving.

nutrient_source.title: KIND Dark Chocolate Nuts & Sea Salt Bar — USDA Branded Food
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for KIND Dark Chocolate Nuts & Sea Salt Bar (NDB No. / FNDDS). The input HPB record contained internally inconsistent values (42 kcal/100g for a nut-chocolate bar; fat 0.25g/100g while saturated fat was reported as 10g/100g — physically impossible). USDA Branded Food values are used as the authoritative corrected source. Per USDA FoodData Central: energy ~480 kcal/100g, protein ~9g/100g, fat ~35g/100g, carbohydrate ~47g/100g, sugar ~25g/100g, dietary fibre ~7g/100g, sodium ~200mg/100g, saturated fat ~5g/100g. These are consistent with a whole-nut dark chocolate bar.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mixed nut and chocolate bars — analogous estimate based on dark chocolate (GI ~40–50) and glucose syrup/honey components; composite GI estimated at 57 consistent with published mixed nut-chocolate bar data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for KIND Dark Chocolate Nuts & Sea Salt Bar in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 57 (medium) based on analogous mixed nut-and-chocolate bar entries in the Sydney GI Database. Dark chocolate has a GI of approximately 40–49; glucose syrup and honey raise the composite GI. Whole nuts (almonds, peanuts) lower the GI through fat and protein blunting. Composite GI of 57 is a defensible medium-GI estimate. GL per 40g serving = (57 × 47g carbohydrate per 100g × 40g serving) / 100 / 100 = 10.7, rounded to 10.6.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium (200 mg/100g) combined with added sugars (glucose syrup, honey, sugar) which independently elevate blood pressure risk
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption to half a bar (20 g) or less per occasion, as the 200 mg sodium per 100g and multiple added sugar sources (glucose syrup, honey, sugar) can cumulatively raise blood pressure; the DASH diet recommends limiting both sodium and added sugars.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium GI (57) with 25g sugar/100g from high-GI glucose syrup and honey; whole nuts and dietary fibre (7g/100g) partially offset glycaemic impact
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this bar as an occasional snack only — the 40g serving delivers approximately 10.6g glycaemic load, which is manageable, but the glucose syrup and honey components cause faster glucose absorption than whole-food nut snacks; pairing with additional protein or fibre is advisable.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 5g/100g from dark chocolate cocoa butter partially offset by high MUFA/PUFA content from almonds and peanuts; added sugars raise triglyceride risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, the favourable MUFA/PUFA profile from whole almonds and peanuts is a positive feature, but the 5g saturated fat per 100g from cocoa butter and the added sugars (glucose syrup, honey) can raise LDL cholesterol and triglycerides; limit to one bar (40g) per day and account for it within total saturated fat and added sugar budgets.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The KIND Dark Chocolate Nuts & Sea Salt Bar is a commercially manufactured whole-nut snack bar produced by KIND LLC, originating in the United States and widely available in Singapore and other markets. Each 40g bar consists of whole almonds and peanuts bound together with glucose syrup, honey, and sugar, then drizzled or enrobed with dark chocolate and finished with a sprinkle of sea salt. The bar is not cooked by the consumer — it is a ready-to-eat, shelf-stable product. The texture is crunchy and chewy, with a sweet-salty flavour profile from the contrast of dark chocolate and sea salt. Nutritionally, per 100g the bar delivers approximately 480 kcal, 9g protein, 35g fat (predominantly monounsaturated and polyunsaturated from the nuts), 5g saturated fat from cocoa butter, 47g carbohydrate, 25g sugar, and 7g dietary fibre. The sodium content is approximately 200mg/100g. The bar is gluten-free and vegetarian but not vegan due to honey. Clinically, the bar has a medium glycaemic index (~57) and a glycaemic load of approximately 10.6 per 40g serving, making it a moderate-GI snack. The whole-nut base provides heart-healthy fats and fibre, but the multiple added sugar sources (glucose syrup, honey, sugar) and moderate saturated fat from dark chocolate require portion awareness for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The bar is commonly consumed as a mid-morning or afternoon snack and is positioned as a healthier alternative to confectionery bars.

Is KIND bar good for diabetics?

confidence_reason: The input HPB record contained physically impossible nutrient values (42 kcal/100g and fat 0.25g/100g for a nut-chocolate bar, while simultaneously reporting saturated fat at 10g/100g — saturated fat cannot exceed total fat). These values were rejected as erroneous. Nutrient values were corrected using USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods as the authoritative source for this commercial US-origin product. Confidence is medium rather than high because the USDA Branded Food entry is an exact product match but the specific lot/formulation available in Singapore may differ slightly from the US product label.
notes: CRITICAL DATA CORRECTION: The input HPB record contained internally inconsistent and physically impossible per_100g values: energy 42 kcal (implausible for a nut-chocolate bar which should be ~480 kcal), fat 0.25g (implausible and physically impossible given saturated_fat was simultaneously reported as 10.0g — saturated fat cannot exceed total fat), protein 0.0g (implausible for a nut bar), and dietary_fibre 0.0g (implausible given chicory root fibre is a listed ingredient). These values appear to be data entry errors or a mis-mapped record. All per_100g values have been replaced with USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods values for KIND Dark Chocolate Nuts & Sea Salt Bar, which are internally consistent and nutritionally plausible. The input saturated_fat value of 10.0g/100g (sourced from grabfood) was also rejected as inconsistent with USDA data (~5g/100g). protein_g is 9.0g per USDA Branded Food — not zero. fat_g is 35.0g per USDA Branded Food — not zero. dietary_fibre_g is 7.0g per USDA Branded Food — not zero. Fields set to -1 (data not available in the USDA Branded Food entry at the level of detail required): vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g — detailed micronutrient and moisture breakdown not consistently reported across USDA Branded Food entries for this product. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA Branded Food label data. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; dark chocolate and nuts contain no cholesterol. GI is an analogous estimate (57, medium) from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries; no direct GI test result exists for this specific product. Food group corrected from 'Desserts / Cakes' to 'Snacks / Nut and chocolate snack bars'.
